original_image_tags: Handwritten signature, appears to read "J. Edgar Hoover"

elements.description: Newspaper clipping with headline 'Juke Box Birthed This Flying Disc, Army Expert Finds'. The text below describes a 'Flying Disc' that fell in Twin Falls, Idaho, manufactured by four teenage boys from parts of a run-down juke box. The article mentions the device was found to be a hoax with a plexiglass dome, three radio tubes, and chromium-plated edges. Below the clipping, text reads 'JUL 12 1947', 'WASHINGTON NEWS', and 'Page 3'.

FBI stamp on the right side of the page reading 'RECORDED' followed by a handwritten number '162-83224-A' and 'FBI' stamped below it, with the date '44 JUL 18 1947' stamped underneath. A separate stamp reads 'EX-74'. A large handwritten signature or annotation is present below these stamps. A date stamp '58 JUL 25 1947' and the number '166' are visible in the bottom left corner.

judge.reason: Description A identifies only a handwritten signature, while Description B describes a newspaper clipping illustration and multiple stamps, indicating they are describing entirely different visual content on the page.
